idea-plugin.name: Submodule Branch Switcher
idea-plugin.description:
One-click switch the main repo and all its submodules to a preset branch combination. Ideal for teams working with microservices or multi-repo projects where branches must stay in sync.

Core workflow:
  • Switch presets in one click — define a branch for the main repo and each submodule, then switch all at once
  • Preflight dry-run preview — table showing current → target branch, dirty file count, and branch source (local/remote) per repo before switching
  • Dirty working tree strategies — stash / skip / force: choose what happens when repos have uncommitted changes; stash auto-pops when switching back
  • Partial failure rollback — checkpoint before switching; one-click rollback on failure
  • Derive feature branches — create feature/xxx on all repos simultaneously from a preset baseline
  • Auto submodule init — missing submodules are auto-initialized after switching
Per-preset overrides (v0.7):
  • Each preset can override global dirty strategy, pull-after-switch, and fetch-before-switch settings
  • Force dirty strategy shows a safety confirmation in both the panel preview and the Ctrl+Alt+B shortcut dialog
Preset management:
  • One-click "snapshot from current state" to create a preset
  • Rename, import/export (clipboard), and search/filter presets
  • Undo last switch from history (last 5 switches)
  • Reorder presets with ↑↓ buttons
Quality:
  • 270 tests, GitHub Actions CI (ubuntu/macOS/Windows), Qodana static analysis
  • i18n: English and Chinese (中文) via IntelliJ DynamicBundle
  • HiDPI-aware, theme-aware (dark/light) UI with IntelliJ native icons
Presets are stored in .idea/branch-presets.json — team members can share via git.

Configure global options at File → Settings → Version Control → Submodule Branch Switcher.

Quick start: open the Submodule Branches tool window, click "From Current State" to snapshot your current branches into a preset, then switch with one click.
